description:
You are assisting a technical-writer who is preparing a “checksums” section
    for the project documentation that lives under /home/user/docs.
    
    A brand-new (empty) placeholder PDF called
    /home/user/docs/manual_v1.2.pdf
    has already been created, but the writer still needs its SHA-256 checksum
    captured in a dedicated log file.

    Goal for the agent
    ------------------
    1. Calculate the SHA-256 checksum of the file
       /home/user/docs/manual_v1.2.pdf .
    2. Append the result to the text file
       /home/user/docs/checksums/sha256sum.txt
       (create the checksums directory and the file if they do not yet exist).

    Exact output format that will be verified
    -----------------------------------------
    • The log file must be named exactly
      /home/user/docs/checksums/sha256sum.txt
      (all lower-case).
    • It must contain a single line **ending with a newline character**.
    • The line must have the exact format produced by the command
      “sha256sum manual_v1.2.pdf”, i.e.

      <64-character-hex-digest><two spaces><original-file-name>\n

      Concretely, when written correctly the line will look like this
      (checksum followed by two spaces and then the file name):

      e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  manual_v1.2.pdf

      The automated test will check:
      • that the two spaces are present between the digest and the filename,
      • that the filename is exactly “manual_v1.2.pdf” (case-sensitive),
      • and that the file ends with a single newline.

    Hints for the agent (do NOT include the commands here)
    ------------------------------------------------------
    • Remember to create the “checksums” directory before writing the file.
    • Make sure to append (>>) so that rerunning the task will not wipe
      existing data, but for this exercise only one line will be present.
    • Verify the contents with “cat” before finishing to ensure correctness.
truth:
Initial system state (already present before the agent starts)
    --------------------------------------------------------------
    • Directory: /home/user/docs
      – permissions: drwxr-xr-x  (owned by user)
      – contents:
        1. manual_v1.2.pdf  (regular file, size 0 bytes)

    Verification data the tests will rely on
    ----------------------------------------
    1. Expected SHA-256 checksum for an empty file:
       e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

    2. After successful completion the file
       /home/user/docs/checksums/sha256sum.txt
       must exist and contain exactly:

       e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  manual_v1.2.pdf\n
name: task_000000_b457cfe1
